My enthusiasm with DevOps and Cloud Native helped me to secure scholarships like Travel Funding Scholarship and Dan Kohn Scholarship by The Linux Foundation and Cloud Native Computing Foundation to attend flagship conferences such as KubeCon + CloudNativeCon + Open Source Summit China 2023 at Shanghai and Open Source Summit Europe 2022 at Dublin. I also worked as an Open Source Developer in Google Summer of Code (GSoC) at DBpedia Association. Apart from my technical experience, I got my hands dirty in fostering the Cloud Native Ecosystem. I am also the Chapter Lead at Cloud Native Hooghly which is an official CNCF Chapter listed amongst 40 such clubs in the Asia Pacific Region. Right now, with the proliferation of the Cloud Native Tools, I successfully turned the chapter to be the fastest growing in the entire Eastern India, catering 250+ Active members. I am also a Sig Contribex's Comms Volunteer for Kubernetes Contributors Summit North America 2023 at Chicago, United States of America primarily looking into the “Call for Proposal” emails and notifying about the updates to the KubeCon co-located event attendees. I have significant experience in pitching tech products to Venture Capitalists and have successfully secured grants from Government of India twice for my deep-tech startup.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I always wanted to boost Cloud Native Ecosystem in my locality whereas I felt there is a gap of awareness. Being an ambassador, it would really help me to reach areas where I couldn't do by myself just being a CNCG chapter lead of Hooghly. With CNCF's support it would be possible.

person.bio: I started my journey in Tech Marketing in 2016, and in 2021, I embarked with SIGHUP on the Cloud Native industry as a complete newbie. For the first six months, I felt overwhelmed by the vastness and complexity of the field, but then something amazing happened along the way – thanks to my stubbornness and the patient support of my colleagues, I actively started contributing to the Cloud Native community and found a perfect place to express my skills while helping others, organizing events, and supporting projects - I'm a KCD Italy and Cloud Native Milano Meetup organizer and part of the Glossary Italian Localization Group. All that apart, I'm music-passionate and a restless shower performer, a winter couch knitter and cat cuddler, a balcony gardening ace, a dabble baker, and a great backwarmer during adventurous motorcycle journeys.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Being a non-tech member and a proud CNCF ambassador, I'm determined to lend my support in any way possible. I'm here to share the lessons I've learned, contribute to the growth of our community, and empower others to discover the Cloud Native Community and find their own path. I want to be a guiding hand to help others navigate CNCF initiatives and projects. It's all about showing that there are different ways to contribute and various subgroups tailored to their expertise.

He also enjoys writing technical blog posts and wrote 10+ blog posts and all of which are published on important official websites including Kyverno, CD Foundation, Flux, Sigstore, etc. • https://cd.foundation/blog/2022/10/18/tekton-chains/ • https://fluxcd.io/blog/2022/10/prove-the-authenticity-of-oci-artifacts/ • https://fluxcd.io/blog/2022/08/manage-kyverno-policies-as-ocirepositories/ • https://fluxcd.io/blog/2022/11/verify-the-integrity-of-the-helm-charts-stored-as-oci-artifacts-before-reconciling-them-with-flux/ • https://kubesimplify.com/the-secret-gems-behind-building-container-images-enter-buildkit-and-docker-buildx • https://blog.kubesimplify.com/getting-started-with-ko-a-fast-container-image-builder-for-your-go-applications • https://nirmata.com/securing_base_images/ • https://blog.sigstore.dev/how-to-verify-container-images-with-kyverno-using-kms-cosign-and-workload-identity-1e07d2b85061Why I am excited to be an ambassador: First of all, I'm the first person from Turkey who has been selected as a CNCF Ambassador which is super great for me because I have the energy to spread all my knowledge with other people and I do really like to collaborate with people by organizing in-person, virtual meetups. Jakob brings a unique blend of technical expertise and passion for cybersecurity to his work. With a strong educational background in computer science and cybersecurity, Jakob has developed a deep understanding of complex security systems and threats. His dedication to staying at the forefront of industry trends and advancements has earned him a reputation as a knowledgeable and effective cybersecurity expert. person.name: Konrad Rzentarzewski
person.bio: I was there when the config burned. In the age before observability, chaos reigned. The servers howled, and nothing scaled but entropy. Then kube cattle were born — simple, nameless beasts born of children’s games. But they multiplied. And their hunger grew vast. Years passed. The sun revolved the Earth. Then the Earth revolved the Cloud. I grew tired of debugging in darkness. I longed for light. And logs. Preferably structured. That’s when I began preaching — not loudly, just… declaratively. One apply at a time. I walked among DevOps tribes, where bash begat shell, and shell begat pain. To them I brought ConfigMaps, and Secrets hidden in plain sight. Then came the mysterious YAML — a language too holy to lint — inscribed deep within the API’s own heart. Through toil and merge, the infrastructure became manifest. Pods floated on the waters. Deployments begat replicas. Helm bore Charts. Git begat Ops. What was once dev spun to prod. And every service had a name — and every name resolved. Unless external DNS was involved. And the Preachers said: \"Behold, our logs streameth clean.\" Thus began the age of the declarative. We once dwelled in darkness. Now, we scale planetary in full light.

Led multidisciplinary teams of up to 100 members through year-long projects, including critical version upgrades and system stabilizations.Why I am excited to be an ambassador:I am deeply passionate about advancing the adoption and understanding of cloud-native technologies, particularly within the Korean technical community. Throughout my professional journey, I have been dedicated to open source advocacy, technical education, and community leadership, with a focus on bridging the gap between global cloud-native advancements and local practitioners. Notably, I have taken a leading role in organizing KCD Seoul and have served as an organizer for the Cloud Native Community Groups (Seoul), effectively acting as an ambassador for CNCF within Korea. Through these activities, I have empowered Korean developers and organizations to fully leverage CNCF projects by providing timely, accessible, and high-quality technical content, workshops, and mentorship programs. I am committed to reducing language and cultural barriers by localizing key resources, fostering inclusive community spaces, and encouraging active participation in global CNCF initiatives. As a CNCF Ambassador, I aim to be a trusted liaison, ensuring that the Korean community not only keeps pace with global trends but also contributes meaningfully to the evolution of the cloud-native ecosystem.

person.name: Doddy Kristianto
person.bio: I have worked in a telecommunications company for almost 19 years at a leading digital telecommunications company in Indonesia. I started my journey in the field of virtualization and cloud technology from 2015 until now. Physical to Virtual (P2V) and Virtual to Virtual (V2V) are the first experiences in virtualization projects that know the basics of cloud computing.  I started planning, assessing and building cloud computing in my company since the end of 2019 by conducting several cloud technology assessment tenders and selecting hyperscalers (cloud provider) according to my company's use cases. our challenge is how we maximize the benefits of cloud computing by migrating monolithic applications to microservices from on-premises to cloud using hyperscaler, with microservices we will have agile delivery to support rapid Go to Market (GTM) from a business point of view, we can support multiple deployments or sprint releases according to the business requirements program every week, it is much easier to maintain our applications / services independently and much more. Therefore, we realize the need to have microservices knowledge by understanding in-depth knowledge of the Kubernetes Platform to support this so that we will have an agile environment that can support our business growth and provide added value and competitive advantage for digital transformation in our company. I started learning Kubernetes from 2022 until now to support all kinds of applications/services migrating to microservices in the cloud. by fully understanding this knowledge we can maximize the value of kubernetes to support our business in our company.

person.bio: DevOps consultant passionate about cloud-native technologies.

person.name: Michael Foster
person.bio: Michael Foster serves as the Community Lead for the open-source StackRox project and as a Senior Principal Technical Marketing Manager at Red Hat, based in Toronto. With a background in containers and Kubernetes adoption for large enterprises, he currently focuses on upskilling security teams to better utilize Kubernetes information for SOC teams. Michael played a pivotal role in creating the Cloud Native Glossary initiative and is an active organizer of CNCF Toronto Meetups. In his free time, he enjoys staying active through skiing, exploring Toronto's on his motorcycle, and tinkering with mechanical projects at home.

person.name: Riccardo Bertelli
person.bio: I've always been amazed by how large companies around the world manage to keep their software-as-a-service platforms running and scaling smoothly. That curiosity pulled me into the world of DevOps, where I instantly fell in love with Docker, Kubernetes, and more recently, NixOS. I’m passionate about cloud-native technologies, automation, and system design, and I’m always exploring how to make systems more resilient and elegant. When I’m not debugging YAML or optimizing CI pipelines, you’ll probably find me explaining to friends why “just one more container” is a perfectly reasonable hobby

person.name: Suryateja Ganta
person.bio: I work as a Principal SRE at Fidelity Investments. My expertise lies in DevOps, GitOps, MLOps and Production Engineering solutions. I primarily use CNCF open source technologies to develop and design robust platforms and solutions for production. I’m part of Container Platform Management team, primarily working on Kubernetes clusters deployed in public (AWS, Azure) and private (on-prem) clouds. Handling around ~650+ cluster including all the Environments. As an active member of many cloud communities and conferences, I love to discuss technical ideas and news.

person.name: Yin Zhang
person.bio: Yin Zhang is an experienced cloud platform engineer based in Melbourne, with over 10 years of hands-on expertise in AWS and a strong focus on cloud-native technologies. Throughout his career, he has developed deep expertise in Kubernetes orchestration and container security, with a passion for building resilient, scalable platforms. As a strong advocate for cloud security best practices, Yin prioritises implementing defense-in-depth strategies across infrastructure, from secure container pipelines to comprehensive access controls and network security. He is committed to advancing the Kubernetes ecosystem and sharing knowledge with the cloud-native community while continuously exploring innovative approaches to secure, efficient platform engineering

person.name: Junichi Miyagawa
person.bio: I work as an IT infrastructure architect and Scrum Master. I have expertise in AWS, Azure, CloudNativeSecurity, Kubernetes, Infrastructure as Code(AWS CDK) and Platform Engineering.

person.name: Mouhamad Koreissi
person.bio: An experienced Sales Engineer | Solutions Architect currently working at Datadog, I have extensive expertise in delivering complex software to small, medium and large organisations using MEDDICC.  My customers have ranged from start-up and mid-market eCommerce companies to French CAC40 organisations in travel industry, financial services and transport.  With a strong interest in Cloud communities, I've been co-leading the Bamako AWS User Group for the past 2 years.  Check out my website to say hi: https://koreissi.com

person.name: Raghu Venkat
person.bio: I am a passionate DevOps/SRE Engineer who love working with complex problem and Kubernetes is my favourite out of everything. I love the security and administration part of Kubernetes.

person.name: Grégory Dusart
person.bio: Experienced cloud architect and senior Java developer with 20+ years in IT, working on AWS and Kubernetes migrations for large-scale enterprise systems. Passionate about re-architecting legacy systems, delivering scalable cloud solutions, and mentoring development teams.

person.name: Jakub Kokoszka
person.bio: I'm Jakub, a DevOps specialist from Poland at Software Mind. My passion lies in Linux, Kubernetes, and networking, where I excel at building robust, scalable solutions. Beyond my technical prowess, I'm known for transforming complex system challenges into elegant, efficient workflows—think of me as the architect who designs bridges where others see chasms. I'm constantly learning and eager to explore new frontiers in IT, driven by a desire to innovate and deliver exceptional results.

person.name: Pepijn Oomen
person.bio: With 40+ years experience in the ICT field, Pepijn has been recognised by IBM as an OS/2 Warp Engineer and a Lotus Notes Principal Engineer, by RedHat as a Certified Engineer (RHCE) and a Certified Specialist in OpenShift and more recently by the Linux Foundation as a Kubestronaut.  He is a long time user of containers and Kubernetes and has previously been providing on-site trainings for RedHat OpenShift.  As a Platform Engineer, Pepijn works with a focus on security, reliability and automation for complex infrastructures.

person.name: Robert Sirchia
person.bio: I create content around CNCF projects, building enablement content around CNCF projects. I help contribute around marketing and community efforts with CNCF projects. I am a new code contributor to a CNCF project

person.name: Chay Te
person.bio: Chay IT background goes from Linux administrator to platform engineer for more than 24 years. He worked for various domain, insurance, bank, public services, telecom, agri-food, pharmaceutical, watchmaking helping people with his passion about automation and containerisation in linux ecosystem as a senior consultant, trainer and coach.  He founds so much fun working in the DevOps area and he likes to share this fun whenever possible.

person.name: Pierre Fort
person.bio: I am a tech enthusiast and IT professional with over 2 decades of experience, I enjoy collaborating with teams and clients on cloud-native solution architecture, DevOps, automation, and observability. I enjoy exploring new technologies and sharing my knowledge with colleagues through mentoring and coaching. The Kubernetes ecosystem is vast and always evolving, and I'm curious to see how AI-related workloads will change the game!

person.name: Taha Dekmak
person.bio: Certified AWS Solutions Architect with expertise in automating and securing development workflows and designing cloud architectures that prioritize security, performance and reliability. Proficient in AWS, Cloudformation, CI/CD, Terraform, Docker and Kubernetes.

person.name: Thor Wei Jie
person.bio: I'm Wei Jie, an AI Developer at SAP. I specialise in Kubernetes, Istio, Knative, Kserve and Argo. I’m passionate about helping developers understand and adopt modern infrastructure tools to make their architecture more scalable and performant.

person.name: David Abrante
person.bio: Senior Systems Engineer with a love for all things containers and open source.

person.name: Rohit Kalva
person.bio: Cloud Architect, DevOps, and Site Reliability Engineer with 10+ years of experience architecting and scaling mission-critical enterprise infrastructure across AWS, GCP, and Azure platforms. I work with container orchestration, cloud-native solutions, AI/ML pipelines, and comprehensive SRE practices including observability and incident management. My focus areas include building resilient containerized environments, implementing DevSecOps practices, Infrastructure as Code, GitOps methodologies, and designing fault-tolerant distributed systems. I'm passionate about open source projects and the Kubernetes ecosystem, and enjoy contributing to the community through shared knowledge and collaborative innovation.

person.name: Matt Slater
person.bio: Experienced Azure Platform Engineer with a passion for anything Kubernetes and Containers.

person.name: Ivan Bruno Puig Dantas
person.bio: SRE and developer with deep expertise across software, infrastructure, and networking. I like understanding systems from the inside out, reading code, spotting subtle issues, and designing systems that are both resilient and secure. Focused on security, malware analysis, and continuously improving how things work behind the scenes.

person.name: Louise Champ
person.bio: I'm a DevOps engineer with nearly 10 years of experience managing production Kubernetes clusters and working with CNCF projects. My work spans architecting and building platforms from scratch through to operational concerns including security, observability, GitOps workflows and pipelines, multi-cluster platform management, developer experience, and cluster lifecycle management, whilst staying engaged with the fast-evolving best practices across the ecosystem. I regularly write technical content exploring practical and timely implementation challenges, and enjoy making complex Kubernetes concepts more accessible.

person.name: Pubudu Perera
person.bio: I’m a seasoned Engineer who has worn many hats over the years as a DevOps, SRE, Platform Engineer and a Developer. I love all things Kubernetes and currently exploring other tools within the CNCF landscape.

person.name: Saravana Pandia Kumar
person.bio: Pandi loves to learn new technologies especially related to Cloud & Containers and use those to solve the customer problems. He is a AWS Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) and Elastic Container Service (ECS) Subject matter expert who on a daily basis interacts with customers solving the problems or guiding them with best architecture/practices. Besides technology, he enjoys running and being with family, friends, coffee and of course beer.

person.name: Hnin Yu Wai
person.bio: I’m passionate about cloud native technologies and Kubernetes with a strong interest in technical solution engineering.  I love exploring how systems work , breaking down complex problems , designing efficient , scalable solutions . Continuous learning and hands-on experimentation motivate me to grow in the cloud ecosystem .

person.name: Nanda Rakesh Gopisetti
person.bio: I am a Senior Cloud DevOps/SRE engineer specializing in Kubernetes, AWS, and CNCF ecosystem tools. I contributes to the community through meetups and LinkedIn posts focused on DevSecOps, GitOps, and cloud-native best practices.

person.name: Piotr Prusaczyk
person.bio: I am a DevOps engineer specializing in Kubernetes and Google Cloud Platform, with expertise in designing scalable solutions and automating workflows. I focus on leveraging cloud-native technologies to optimize system performance and reliability.  Beyond my professional interests, I am passionate about classical music, which inspires my focus and creativity in both work and life.

person.name: Faseela K
person.bio: Faseela is a cloud-native developer, maintainer and steering committee member at Istio Service Mesh. She has served as the Program Chair for the Istio Meetups in 2023 including the IstioDays co-located with KubeCon Europe and NorthAmerica. She regularly contributes to the Istio blogs, helping all the latest news about Istio reach users and contributors worldwide.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Having done the work of an ambassador for Istio for several years now, I look forward to continuing this work officially now, making use of the support CNCF provides. I would love to provide more support for the women in cloud native community with this new opportunity I am provided with.

person.name: Kevin Tijssen
person.bio: Kevin Tijssen is a Cloud Native Engineer at Bright Cubes, specializing in Kubernetes, Infrastructure-as-Code, pipeline automation, and cloud technologies. With a wealth of experience in Cloud Native and DevOps roles, Kevin brings valuable expertise to Fullstaq. He is dedicated to driving cloud-native transformations and enhancing operational excellence.

person.name: Krzysztof Greczka
person.bio: I'm a dedicated professional with a passion for cloud and services-oriented solutions. For me, the most developing aspect of work is the opportunity to learn from experts and share my knowledge with others. I believe that open discussions about solutions and experiences are crucial at every stage of a project, adding value to both the endeavor and the individuals involved.

person.name: Maryam Tavakkoli
person.bio: Maryam Tavakkoli is a senior Cloud engineer at RELEX Solutions, specializing in the design and implementation of Cloud and Kubernetes infrastructure. She is also one of the organizers of the CNCF meetups in Finland. With a strong passion for open-source cloud-native solutions, Maryam actively engages in collaborative efforts and idea-sharing within this domain. Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I've had the fortune to learn from world-class cloud native leaders, and I want to pay it forward by organizing, speaking at and participating in cloud native events. In addition, I'm excited to make the cloud native concepts more tangible through my research.

person.name: Meliani Abdelghani
person.bio: PhD student at EURECOM focusing on edge-to-cloud systems. Passionate about software engineering, especially the runtime layer of cloud-native applications and container technologies. Previous work includes proactive migration of stateful applications and automation of Kubernetes cluster creation and scaling.

person.name: Min-Jiun Tsai
person.bio: As a Machine Learning Engineer with three years of experience in the semiconductor manufacturing industry, I specialize in developing AI/ML solutions focused on optimizing product quality. Recognizing the growing demand for scalable and efficient deployment strategies, I began learning Kubernetes (K8s), understanding its significant role in modernizing cloud-native infrastructures. My goal is to deliver scalable, automated, and reliable AI/ML solutions that drive continuous innovation and improve operational performance. I would like to express my gratitude to the mentors and peers who have supported me throughout my preparation for the Kubernetes certification. I look forward to achieving greater breakthroughs in the cloud-native ecosystem and advancing AI/ML integration with the broader community.

person.name: Muhaned Yahya
person.bio: I’m a DevOps engineer who applies cloud-native principles to automate complex operational tasks and build scalable, reliable, and resilient systems. I focus on creating Kubernetes-based developer platforms that improve consistency, security, and developer experience. I’m the creator of KubeUser (https://github.com/openkube-hub/KubeUser), an open-source project built to solve real-world Kubernetes access and user management challenges. I regularly share practical insights with the cloud-native community on LinkedIn and Medium (https://medium.com/@yahya.muhaned).

person.name: Shao Wang
person.bio: I am a DevOps Engineer at Delta Electronics, focusing on managing our Kubernetes platforms. I really enjoy learning about cloud-native tools and solving technical challenges every day. I am excited to join this community and keep growing with other Kubernetes fans.

person.name: Jonas Keller
person.bio: As a Cloud Engineer, I specialise in building on-premise cloud-native container platforms with a strong focus on platform and infrastructure automation as well as GitOps practices. My background spans networking, compute, storage, and virtualization across major vendors in the enterprise IT landscape. I aim to bridge the gap between hardware and software by building scalable, efficient, and reliable platforms that support modern application delivery.

person.name: Thomas Vaillant
person.bio: I’m a DevOps consultant with 10+ years helping teams ship reliable, cloud‑native software in high‑scale and regulated environments. I started as a full‑stack developer before specializing in DevOps, so I naturally bridge app code, infrastructure, and developer experience. Day to day you’ll find me on Kubernetes, Terraform, CI/CD, and observability; and I’m happy to jump into Go or TypeScript when it helps. I’ve led SRE teams and migrations from legacy to the cloud with a focus on security and pragmatic business impact. I love contributing to open source and leaving teams more autonomous than when I arrived.

person.name: Wiriyang Pipatsakulroj
person.bio: Wiriyang is a customer engineer at Google Cloud, passionate about performance computing and cloud technologies. His interests focus on workload scheduling, observability, and service mesh projects within the CNCF ecosystem.

person.name: Anastasiia Gubska
person.bio: Anastasiia is a Lead Software Engineer at Chase UK, focusing on cloud-native ecosystems and software delivery best practices. She enjoys sharing practical insights on Argo and organizes the Deaf in Cloud Native meetup group, focused on empowering Deaf and Hard of Hearing professionals in the tech industry. Through her involvement in the Deaf and Hard of Hearing Working Group (DHHWG), she has contributed to best practices for inclusive, welcoming conferences, authored the interviewing DHH guide for the CNCF Accessibility chapter, and collaborates with the LF/CNCF to enhance accessibility across the ecosystem.

person.name: Christian Pedersen
person.bio: Graduated with a PhD in Physics and Nanotechnology in 2020, working with Linux and Python on a supercomputer cluster. Worked in IT since early 2021 in a senior data engineer role with a cloud engineering focus. Transferred to current employment as senior data engineer in early 2025, still with a strong focus on cloud-native tools.

person.name: Manh Pham
person.bio: Backend Software Engineer & Open Source Enthusiast with a strong interest in the CNCF ecosystem. I enjoy working with Kubernetes-based systems and studying the internals of cloud-native projects to better understand their behavior in real-world environments. I actively engage with the community and have participated in CNCF and Linux Foundation events, including the Kubernetes 10th Birthday Bash in Ho Chi Minh City. My focus is on building scalable, reliable backend services and continuously learning from open-source communities.

person.name: Spas Atanasov
person.bio: Spas Atanasov is a Manager of Site Reliability Engineering at MariaDB with more than 10 years of experience working with Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. He is a Co-organizer of the Cloud Native Meetups in Bulgaria since 2018, where the community has grown from 20 to over 2000 members, and a Co-organizer of Kubernetes Community Days Sofia. He speaks at meetups and actively mentors people, helping them gain the confidence to step on stage and share their knowledge. His focus areas include Kubernetes at scale, SRE, Cloud Native, Observability, and Cost Optimization. He strongly believes in the power of community and the idea that sharing knowledge can change the way people think and work. Based in Sofia, Bulgaria, Spas enjoys baking sourdough bread, making barbecues, and cooking huge stacks of pancakes – but most of all, spending time with his family. He is also known for his favorite question: "Why?". Why he is motivated: "I love seeing the spark of satisfaction in people's eyes when I have helped them learn something or see things from a different perspective."

person.name: Ileriayo Adebiyi
person.bio: Ileriayo is a Certified Kubernetes Developer, Administrator, and Security Specialist (CKAD, CKA, CKS) with a passion for people and technology. Co-organizer of the CNCF Lagos community group and KCD Nigeria committed to the growth of Africa. A teacher who takes pleasure in democratizing complex technical concepts. He is the creator of Get Going With Kubernetes, which consists of book reading and a series of workshops to help people learn Kubernetes.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: The adoption of Cloud Native in Africa has been quite low compared to other regions. Becoming an ambassador provides me the opportunity to champion the adoption, development, and growth of Cloud Native on a bigger scale in Africa.Sessionize: https://sessionize.com/ileriayo

person.name: Kerim Kocabaş
person.bio: I am a DevOps Engineer/SRE and a novice software developer. On the open source front I usually build tech that is useful to me (and maybe others) including Kubernetes tools (https://github.com/bouquet2) and a Linux distribution (see https://linux.krea.to). I mainly am focusing on Kubernetes and OpenTofu nowadays. I don't write much blog posts but it is available at https://krea.to/blog

person.name: Pratibha Panditrao Dive
person.bio: Senior DevOps/Cloud Engineer with 8+ years of experience architecting and operating Kubernetes-based platforms across AWS, GCP, and on-premise environments. Proficient in cluster administration, infrastructure as code using Terraform, and building secure, scalable CI/CD pipelines with GitLab and Azure DevOps. Experienced in managing production workloads on Amazon EKS and GKE, implementing service mesh for secure service-to-service communication, and leading Kubernetes platform migrations from on-premise to cloud. Passionate about improving system reliability, security, and scalability through automation, observability, and modern cloud-native practices.

person.name: Balaji Kannadassan
person.bio: Senior Software Engineer with over 19 years of experience designing and operating large‑scale, cloud‑native telecom systems. Specializes in Kubernetes, CNCF technologies, and distributed systems, with deep hands‑on expertise in resiliency and software upgrade strategies.

person.name: Guillaume Mailhol
person.bio: Young platform engineer working on innovation and improving developer experience. Passionate about new technologies and cloud native. Love to go to conferences like Kubecon, Fosdem, etc and local meetup. Rust && WASM Enthusiast. Strong belief in Kubernetes and cloud native as bests standards for platform engineering.

person.name: Matthew Cascio
person.bio: Digital product leader. Speaker, author, advisor. KDC organizer in Washington, DC and Medellín, Colombia. KCD fiscal sponsor through my company NāOM Lab.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Excited to promote CNCF projects, initiatives and culture through community events and meetups.

person.name: Oleg Franko
person.bio: I'm a System and DevOps Engineer, passionate about the ethical values and freedom of Open Source Software, upholding its origins and independence, especially in the GNU/Linux and Kubernetes ecosystem.

person.name: Blessing Phiri
person.bio: Blessing Phiri is a platform and infrastructure engineer with a strong Linux foundation and hands-on experience across Kubernetes, cloud platforms, and automation. He is a Red Hat Certified Architect (RHCA) and CNCF Kubestronaut, with a focus on designing reliable, scalable, and cost-aware platforms for modern workloads. His interests span Kubernetes internals, container orchestration, OpenShift, GitOps, and AI infrastructure. Blessing is passionate about sharing practical, hands-on knowledge through labs, community discussions, and technical writing.

person.name: Jon Zeolla
person.bio: Jon Zeolla is the founder of Zenable, where they are revolutionizing corporate governance by integrating cloud-native security and compliance with artificial intelligence. Previously he co-founded Seiso where he received the 2021 Start-Up Innovator of the Year Award and currently serves as strategic advisor, guiding key business decisions and company growth.He is also a CNCF Ambassador, SANS Instructor for SEC540: Cloud Security and DevSecOps Automation, and an active member of CNCF TAG Security.

person.name: Kadir Taşkıran
person.bio: I’m a DevOps/Cloud and Platform Engineer with a strong focus on Kubernetes, container orchestration, GitOps, and modern CI/CD practices. I work extensively with CNCF technologies to design secure, scalable, and observable cloud-native platforms, with a particular interest in software supply chain security and Kubernetes security. I enjoy building automation-driven infrastructure using tools like Kubernetes, Helm, Terraform, and GitOps workflows. I’m also passionate about sharing knowledge, mentoring engineers, and continuously exploring emerging patterns in the cloud-native ecosystem.

person.name: Khaja Ehteshamuddin Ahmed
person.bio: A highly motivated DevSecOps engineer with a strong background in cloud computing and automation, I excel at building and managing scalable infrastructure. I have a particular interest in CNCF technologies, including Kubernetes, Prometheus, and Helm, and utilize Terraform for infrastructure as code. I am committed to knowledge sharing and actively participate in the DevOps community.
person.location: Al Nahda Sharjah, United Arab Emirates

person.name: Mateus Muller
person.bio: Mateus is a Senior DevOps Engineer at Zeta Global, where he uses CNCF tools and automation to enable developers to deliver good software, faster and more secure. Passionate about content creation, has also runs a blog and an youtube channel to share knowledge with Brazilian folks.  https://mateusmuller.me/ https://www.youtube.com/c/MateusMuller
person.location: Campo Bom, Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil

person.name: Mauritz Uphoff
person.bio: Cloud Solution Architect who is fascinated by the beauty of Kubernetes

person.name: Mahdi Hamdi
person.bio: I’m a DevOps and Cloud Engineer passionate about cloud-native technologies and automation. I specialize in AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform, and CI/CD, building scalable and secure infrastructures for enterprise environments.

person.name: Gaurav Bansal
person.bio: CNCF enthusiast with eagerness to learn different technologies under CNCF umbrella. Participate in various CNCF Calgary meetups and planning to host couple in the upcoming months. Specialist in Kubernetes, Security, Observability with special focus on automated build management systems

person.name: Jungil Choi
person.bio: Hello! I'm a cloud architect at CJ OliveNetworks, where I take charge of public cloud consulting, infrastructure design, construction, and operations.  I'm passionate about Kubernetes and CNCF technologies, and I strive to turn that passion into meaningful impact for both my team and the broader tech ecosystem.  I'm thrilled to join the Kubestronaut community! I look forward to sharing insights, learning from fellow explorers, and pushing the boundaries of cloud-native innovation together.  I’ll continue doing my best to ensure that my IT knowledge and skills — including Kubernetes — create value both internally and beyond. 🚀

person.name: Brandt Keller
person.bio: Passionate about Open Source, mentorship, and community building through engagement with diverse groups and perspectives. Member of TAG Security and multiple CNCF working groups. Currently specializing in cloud native policy and compliance.

person.name: Sanjay Sachdev
person.bio: Hi there, I am Sanjay Sachdev and I work at Red Hat as a Senior Cloud Consultant. I work in a customer facing, pre-sales and post-sales solution designing role at Red Hat, primarily focusing on customer adoption of Kubernetes and other cloud-native technologies. In my free time I like to write blogs on the technologies and tools that I have been exploring. Following is a link to my blog posts - https://medium.com/@ssachdev-pros

person.name: Suh Junmin
person.bio: I’m a backend engineer building an IaaS cloud platform, and I lead a team focused on platform reliability and operational excellence.  I’m particularly interested in observability and automation to reduce operational toil.

person.name: Vladan Divac
person.bio: Passionate about securing cloud-native ecosystems, driving secure DevOps, and empowering businesses to innovate safely and confidently in the cloud — at scale.

person.name: Aditya Sundaramurthy
person.bio: I'm Aditya, a platform engineer with 18 years of experience in architecting and building enterprise projects and distributed cloud computing products, as well as large Kubernetes-based internal developer platforms. I'm passionate about open-source software and developer experience, and have worked with Kubernetes and CNCF landscape tooling since 2016. During my spare time, I like to read, travel, tinker with my homelab and participate in the local CNCF meetup scene.

person.name: Cemal Yıldırım Dalar
person.bio: Entrepreneur | co-founder gittigidiyor.com | Kubestranaut | Cloud Architect ☁️I'm a Cloud Native Engineer and Kubestronaut based in Amsterdam, and co-founder and former CTO of GittiGidiyor.com (acquired by eBay). With deep experience in building scalable platforms, I now focus on Kubernetes and CNCF technologies to help teams deploy and manage modern infrastructure. I'm passionate about open-source, automation, and mentoring the next generation of engineers.

person.name: Abdelati Elasri
person.bio: Site Reliability Engineer with special interest in Kubernetes, GitOps, and the cloud-native ecosystem. Focused on CI/CD, and building scalable and secure infrastructure. Active member of the Moroccan tech community through Geeksblabla and Devsinstyle.

person.name: Adriano Bubnjek
person.bio: DevOps Engineer from Croatia with expertise in Kubernetes, OpenShift, and Linux systems, currently working at Combis in the Open Source team. Specialized in designing and administering cloud-native infrastructures for industries such as government, banking, insurance, and finance. Experienced with automation tools including Ansible, as well as container backup and restore solutions like Kasten. Skilled across the Red Hat technology portfolio and knowledgeable in cloud platforms such as OpenStack, AWS, and Azure, with a focus on building reliable, scalable, and secure enterprise platforms.

person.name: Hung Fai Leung
person.bio: Tech lover. Enjoys learning new things. Has a strong interest in databases and platform engineering.

person.name: Oluebube Princess Egbuna
person.bio: Oluebube Princess, also Bube is a Devrel Engineer and an advocate for cloud-native technologies.  She co-leads the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) Abuja Chapter and is currently the Docs sub-lead for the Kubernetes v1.31 release. Her expertise lies in container orchestration with Kubernetes and technologies around orchestration.

person.name: Gerhard Häring
person.bio: With a background in software engineering, my focus has shifted towards cloud and cloud-native technology. I am very passionate about making organizations and teams get the most value out of their cloud setup. Here, there is so much more potential for faster iterations, better efficiency and cost savings!

person.name: Katherine Hughes
person.bio: Katherine brings over a decade of experience in event management, with a strong background in planning and executing corporate and nonprofit conferences and events of all sizes across a wide range of industries and destinations. She has a proven track record in contract negotiation, event logistics, and production management. Detail-oriented and creativity-driven, Katherine thrives on juggling complex logistics, solving problems in real time, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver seamless, high-impact experiences. She’s always looking for ways to improve efficiency, elevate attendee engagement, and exceed expectations.

person.name: Igor De Beijer
person.bio: Software Engineer and a builder at heart with a passion for platform engineering. I build with and on top of Kubernetes, focusing on platforms, operators, and extending the K8s API. I maintain a little tool to juggle k8s contexts: https://github.com/idebeijer/kubert. Occasionally, I like to write at https://debeijer.io/blog.
person.location: Millingen aan de Rijn, Gelderland, Netherlands

person.name: Joffrey Janiec
person.bio: Freelance DevOps/SRE with 7 years of experience, who started in development before focusing on cloud and infrastructure for startups & big companies. I like to build resilient architectures with Kubernetes, always with a strong focus on developer experience.

person.name: Leonard Pahlke
person.bio: Hey, my name is Leonard Pahlke, and I am a software engineer with a focus on cloud, open source and sustainability. I like to build systems that tend to get big and complex. I post regularly on the CNCF blog and my own website lvsp.io. I am a CNCF Ambassador, Chair for the CNCF TAG Environmental Sustainability, lead the Kubernetes release team for v1.26 and organize meetups in Hamburg.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I love the cloud and the infinite variety and complexity of systems that can be built on it. The CNCF truly makes cloud-native applications accessible to anyone and any platform. The open source spirit fascinates and inspires me. Serving as a ambassador the community is a great honor.

person.name: Prabhu Jayakumar
person.bio: I am a Software Engineer passionate about building scalable and reliable Kubernetes and cloud platform infrastructure, with a strong DevOps and SRE focus. I bring 13+ years of industry experience, including 7+ years specializing in Kubernetes and cloud‑native technologies. I have led provisioning and scale testing of 1,000+ Kubernetes clusters, with deep expertise in Cluster API and CNCF tools such as Argo CD, Prometheus, and Grafana. I have designed and operated large‑scale Kubernetes platforms across Azure, AWS, and GCP, focusing on reliability, automation, cost optimization, and observability

person.name: Tolga Karaoglan
person.bio: I'm a Platform Engineer/SRE at RELEX Solutions.  My job, in a nutshell: making sure infrastructure stays out of the way so developers can focus on what actually matters. I live in the chaotic world of streaming data, cloud infrastructure, and distributed systems. What excites me most is figuring out how to make complex systems more approachable—without pretending the complexity isn't there. I'm endlessly curious, always learning, security and I genuinely enjoy sharing what I pick up along the way.

person.name: Andrew Aiken
person.bio: I'm a practicing DevOps and Cloud Security Engineer who works heavily with Kubernetes, infrastructure as code & AWS. I'm a volunteer for the NECCDL (neccdl.org) which sets up cyber defense competitions for college students across the northeast of the USA. Outside of work, I am usually either working on a project, spending time outside, or studying.

person.name: Mendjargal
person.bio: I am a DevOps Engineer based in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia, dedicated to building robust cloud-native environments. My core expertise lies in Infrastructure as Code (laC), Kubernetes, and AWS Cloud, with a strong emphasis on security, network security, and automation for critical infrastructure.

person.name: Ricardo Carrillo Cruz
person.bio: Ricardo Carrillo Cruz is a Principal Software Engineer at Red Hat, where he serves as Tech Lead and Architect for Installer/Productization in the Ansible organization. With 20 years of experience in cloud and automation, he has deep expertise across the full evolution of the industry. He previously worked on the OpenShift Networking and Telco RAN Engineering teams, bringing strong Kubernetes and cloud-native telecommunications knowledge to his work. A dedicated open-source contributor, he served as an OpenStack Infra core contributor and Ansible OpenStack modules maintainer. Outside of work, he's a devoted father who loves spending time with his wife and three kids, traveling, and enjoying good food.

person.name: Aleksandar Ivanov
person.bio: I have been an IT professional since 2012. Started as a frontend developer in the e-commerce field with the typical for the time HTML/CSS/JS/jQuery stack. In 2019 naturally I switched to DevOps after I realized that without even knowing I was implementing a variety of DevOps practices in my team. I am currently pursuing Cloud specializations with a main focus on the development lifecycle with Kubernetes, predominantly utilizing CNCF-graduate and other OSS projects like VS Code, Lens, Terraform, Helm, Argo, Istio, Prometheus, Trivy etc. Outside of the digital world I am a philosophy and singing enthusiast, as well as a mental health advocate.

person.name: Constanze Roedig
person.bio: Key Researcher & Lecturer, Public Speaker, Creator of the Software Bill of Behavior (SBoB) and maintainer of the Kubernetes Storm Center for cloudnative intelligence Backstory: Living intentionally is hard when routine takes control. I grew up wanting to be a scientist, studying Biochemistry, starting med school, and ultimately earning a PhD in General Relativity to explore how humans perceive 'reality.' When US funding cuts ended that path, I realized academia left me without agency.In the private sector, I discovered the value of risk. The Cloud Native paradigm let me experiment, fail fast, and try again without fear of lasting damage. In positive-sum collaborations, I saw how my algorithms could shape real-world outcomes, and how taking calculated risks created visible change for people and systems alike. This freedom to test ideas with relatively low cost revealed a new kind of agency, an immediacy and measurable connection to the meaning of work.My vision is to empower others to leverage cloud native technology with respect for the human element, security, and risk. Because having agency means creating reality for our society, for each one of us, one epsilon at a time.

Before his open source career, Jonathan spent over a decade in data center infrastructure and co-founded one of the industry's earliest cloud computing companies, which grew into a billion-dollar business following its acquisition by Rackspace. Passionate about open collaboration, infrastructure innovation, and empowering users, contributors, and ecosystems worldwide, Jonathan frequently speaks at international conferences on open source strategy, infrastructure modernization, cloud-native technologies, and open source AI initiatives.

I’m currently studying GitOps with Argo CD, and I’m Red Hat certified (RHCSA, RHCE, Red Hat Certified Specialist in Containers, and Red Hat Certified OpenShift Administrator) with an infrastructure background in OpenStack and VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF).

I have a strong background in platform engineering, building robust systems that empower teams to deliver software efficiently and securely.

person.name: Robert Klonner

person.name: Tobias Lahrmann Hansen
person.bio: Tobias is a seasoned Linux and Cloud Native enthusiast with hands-on experience running Kubernetes clusters on-premise and in the cloud. His practical knowledge and adaptability in these environments make him a valuable asset to any tech team.  While enjoying his advanced Linux computer janitor role, he slowly got tired of the toil involved with hand-rolling new servers and juggling shared libraries. Seeking a better way to bin-pack applications, he began advocating the use of containers; however, this didn't remove the need to hand-roll servers, so his journey led him to explore Kubernetes, where he quickly gained a taste for monitoring workloads with Prometheus and empowering his colleagues to make metrics-driven decisions.  Having worked with Kubernetes and its abstractions, Tobias still prefers his simple command line for most tasks. He firmly believes in the UNIX philosophy and KISS principles, even if daily driving Emacs might go against these principles.

person.name: Tommy Duc Luu

person.name: Belhedi Chaima

I’m especially drawn to Kubernetes, Infrastructure as Code, cloud automation, security, and the ever-evolving cloud ecosystem. I love building reliable, scalable systems and finding joy in the small details that make big things work.  Currently, I’m a Platform Engineer at InstaDeep, where collaboration, growth, and lifting each other up are at the heart of what we do. I’m incredibly grateful to the mentors and teammates who’ve supported, challenged, and believed in me—your guidance has shaped my journey.  If there’s any way I can help, contribute, or even exchange knowledge—don’t hesitate to reach out. I’m always happy to share, support, and keep learning—together.

person.bio: Linux, Networking and Go

His current work bridges cryptography, system architecture, and operational security.

With Kevin Evans, I run the StreamingClouds live stream, where we engage with inspiring individuals—our industry’s thought leaders—spreading positivity and sharing their knowledge and advice. And the StreamingClouds Community is a safe place where we’re helping each other, supported by a fantastic group of moderators we can call friends.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I’m really excited about joining the CNCF on the mission of "making cloud native ubiquitous". Enhancing my efforts of leading by example, inspiring and helping as many people as possible while being open and inclusive, and treating everyone with the utmost respect. Making a difference through community building and mentoring, impacting the lives of others, leveling the playing field, and fostering community collaboration and relationships. And last but not least, the technologist in me loves cloud-native!

My goal is to advance Kubernetes best practices and help organizations build reliable, secure, and scalable systems.

Márk also organizes several tech meetups in Hungary (Go Budapest, Kubernetes and Cloud Native Budapest, Microservices Budapest) and regularly appears as a speaker in the region at meetups, workshops, conferences and podcasts. You can occasionally find him posting to his blog (https://sagikazarmark.hu/), but chances are his GitHub profile is more interesting. In his free time, Márk likes riding a bicycle, reading books, but his favorite hobby for more than 20 years has been Hungarian folk dance.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I always liked learning/building new technologies and then sharing the knowledge with others. By working on various Open Source projects for more than a decade I also learned to appreciate the opportunity to learn from people coming from different backgrounds. By being an ambassador I hope that I'll have more opportunities to connect with people, learn from them and maybe share some of my knowledge as well.

What began as simple curiosity quickly turned into a serious commitment. I stayed up late many nights, pushing through exhaustion, confusion, and countless errors while trying to understand complex Kubernetes concepts. In just over four months, all that effort paid off when I became a Kubestronaut at the age of 19! The journey so far wasn't easy, but it strengthened my discipline and deepened my passion for cloud-native technologies. Today, I continue to build on that foundation by improving my programming skills, exploring modern infrastructure and software development, and diving into emerging areas such as AI and Agentic AI.

person.name: Khalid Shaheen

person.name: Tijmen Van Den Brink
person.bio: As a Lead Solution Architect I design cloud native platforms that enable organizations to run critical workloads securely and reliably. Over the past five years, I played a key role in developing a private cloud platform for the dutch government—from winning the tender through design, implementation, and operations. Building on this foundation, I'm now driving our Cloud Native portfolio, including Kubernetes as a Service and S3 Object Storage (AIstor/MinIO). My current focus is scaling these offerings across our client base. Together with my international team, we're strengthening clients' security posture through expanded services: policy enforcement with Kyverno, container runtime protection, CVE scanning, and ArgoCD for GitOps delivery. With an MSc in System and Network Engineering from the University of Amsterdam, I specialize in platform engineering and transforming successful client-specific solutions into scalable product offerings. My approach combines hands-on technical expertise with portfolio-level thinking—ensuring individual solutions benefit the wider organization.Technical focus areas: Platform as a Product (Kubernetes as a Service, Object Storage), Container security and policy enforcement (Kyverno), GitOps and CI/CD (ArgoCD, Flux) Observability (Grafana, Prometheus). What drives me is bridging the gap between client-specific innovation and reusable platform services—taking what works at scale for one organization and evolving it into offerings that deliver value across multiple contexts.

person.name: Vinod Sr
person.bio: I'm a Platform Engineer with a strong focus on Kubernetes, cloud-native platforms, and the evolving intersection of AI and infrastructure. I serve as the lead organizer of CNCF London – Westminster, where I help build a thriving community around cloud-native technologies. Passionate about knowledge sharing, I regularly post insights on LinkedIn and contribute to open discussions around DevOps, platform engineering, and AI

person.name: Clifford Imaguezegie
person.bio: A Telecommunications Executive and Technology Leader with over 16 years of experience architecting large-scale network transformations. As a CNCF Kubestronaut, he bridges the gap between traditional 4G/5G RAN infrastructures and modern cloud-native ecosystems to drive automation and operational excellence. Clifford is dedicated to leveraging Kubernetes to redefine the future of Open RAN and telecommunications at a global scale.

person.name: Lincoln Coutinho Alves
person.bio: A lover of challenges, I started developing codes in 2009. Golang specialist and enthusiast. I have solid knowledge in Scala, PHP, Python, Ruby (With proven experience in these languages). Post graduated in Software Engineering, and Bachelor in Computer Science, always looking to update myself with new technologies.  I have over 15 years of experience in technology (working in technical leadership, DevOps/DexEx, programming languages).  I have also worked as a university professor and possess a strong knowledge of DevOps/DevEx (acting as a leader of platform teams (with a focus on DevOps and DevExperience), and Cloud Security).

person.name: Nguyen Tien Minh
person.bio: As a software engineer based in Tokyo Japan, he spends most of the daytime on Kubernetes YAML, Groovy script, TypeScript to build application platform that serves out over millions of clients reliably. His interests lie on cloud and distributed system.

person.name: Piotr Reszke
person.bio: Piotr have over 12 years of experience in the industry, majority of it in DevOps path. Specialises in Kubernetes and AWS. On daily basis he helps companies to get their infra setup and processes to the next level.

person.name: Stanislav Cherkasov
person.bio: I’m Stanislav Cherkasov, a Senior DevSecOps and Platform Engineer based in Poland with 10+ years of experience building secure, production-grade cloud-native platforms across AWS, Azure, and GCP. I specialize in Kubernetes security and platform engineering, GitOps, Iac and Architecture, with a strong focus on automation, reliability, and audit-ready delivery pipelines. I share hands-on notes, exam write-ups, and technical deep-dives and my homelab on my blog: https://tenhishadow.github.io/about
person.location: Czarnochowice, Gmina Wieliczka, Wieliczka County, Lesser Poland Voivodeship, Poland

person.name: Jayton Tjon
person.bio: Communicative DevOps Engineer with over 20 years of experience in IT, designing and improving system infrastructures.  Mostly focused on Kubernetes and CNCF technologies, whether they run on premise or in public cloud, I just love solving problems for my customers!

person.name: Zain Ul Abideen
person.bio: I'm a Platform Engineer working extensively with Red Hat OpenShif and Openshift Virtualization, focusing on production-grade deployments and observability using the ELK stack with bi-directional Cross-Cluster Replication (CCR).I’m actively exploring CNCF programs and contributing to building resilient, scalable platforms. With a strong interest in CNCF technologies, I actively pursue certifications, stay engaged with the community, and promote best practices across hybrid and on-prem infrastructures

person.name: Prashant Lakhera
person.bio: I'm an expert in Cloud and DevOps. I hold many technical certifications that show my deep understanding of AWS Cloud, DevOps, Kubernetes, Python and Shell Automation, and Linux system administration, along with problem- solving skills like debugging, troubleshooting, and performance tuning.

person.name: Takeshi Miyazawa
person.bio: I work as a devops engineer and cloud architect specializing in cloud native applications. I have been involved in the design, development and operation of applications using Kubernetes.

person.name: Adrian Simion Vlad
person.bio: Experienced consultant in multiple areas from virtualization to cybersecurity/ cloud and microservices solutions in public/ private and fintech startup environments. 19+ years working as am architect with hands-on expertise 120+ certifications

person.name: Jeremy Garcia
person.bio: Jeremy is the VP of technical community and open source at Datadog, where he's responsible for developer advocacy, training, documentation, technical writing, multiple engineering teams, and the open source programs office. An ardent but realistic open source advocate with a pragmatic approach, he is the founder of LinuxQuestions.org, which is one of the largest independent open source communities on the web. An early podcaster, he's currently a presenter on Bad Voltage. He's also on the board of the Linux Fund. Before joining Datadog Jeremy ran a consultancy helping organizations implement open source programs, find viable open source business models, and grow technical communities. He's spoken at a variety of conferences, had a column in Linux Magazine, was an ambassador for Opensource.com, and regularly blogged about the intersection of business and open source. Outside of technology he is an avid runner and foodie, and long time Buffalo Bills fan.

person.name: Sebastian Zawadzki
person.bio: I’m a systems engineer at heart who loves solving problems through code. I’m big on Kubernetes, automation, and observability - anything that helps systems run smarter and more reliably. I care deeply about doing things the right way, with security always top of mind.

person.name: Thomas Johansen
person.bio: Coming from a VMware operations/administration/automation background I have been working with kubernetes for the past 2 years and I finally have the proper knowledge and confidence in manouvering around in the bewildering world of cloud native.

person.name: Artur Propp
person.bio: I'm a passionate software engineer with a background in industrial engineering. I love building the full stack including the infrastructure for web and mobile apps that scale. To achieve this, I keep on learning and exploring new technology stacks.

person.name: José Ureba Camacho
person.bio: Driven by a naturally curious mind, I am a cloud-native enthusiast specializing in CNCF technologies, with expertise in Kubernetes, observability, and security. I am dedicated to building secure and scalable cloud-native applications and infrastructure, with a strong commitment to DevOps practices and seamless automation. Beyond technology, I have a deep passion for music, cinema, and literature.

person.name: Marwan Eltawashi
person.bio: I am Cloud automation lead in a leading bank in the UAE,  i have previously started my career with datacenter virtualization and for the last few years i have transitioned to containers, Kubernetes and cloud native technologies

person.name: Noe Thalheim
person.bio: Passionate troubleshooter, diving deep into complex systems to untangle and improve them.

person.name: Kashi Viswanathan
person.bio: I have expertise in various cloud and related technologies, including but not limited to Azure, Kubernetes, and Terraform. I work in a Cloud Engineering Team, maintaining a critical pension administration system, ensuring seamless operations with a focus on availability and scalability. Passionate about continuous learning, I am always eager to explore new technologies and further enhance my skills.

person.name: Pranesh Venkatraman
person.bio: I’m a Cloud Security Engineer at Apple, passionate about creating secure and scalable solutions to help drive innovation in the cloud.

person.name: Sebastian Scheele
person.bio:
Sebastian Scheele is CEO and co-founder of Kubermatic, focused on developing open core software solutions for automating Kubernetes operations, at scale. Sebastian, a developer himself, has his finger on the pulse of where the Kubernetes landscape is going and is contributing many of his team's resources to upstream K8s development. As one of the early pioneers in the cloud native ecosystem, he has helped hundreds of enterprises embrace Kubernetes to solve cutting edge challenges in both cloud and edge computing.

Sebastian speaks at numerous conferences around the world about the impact of Kubernetes and cloud native technologies for IT.

person.name: Shabbir Saifee
person.bio: I am a Senior SRE at McKesson, specializing in Kubernetes and CNCF technologies.  I create Kubernetes and CNCF tutorials on my YouTube channel 'CookNCode'.

person.name: Jamal Ait Ahmed
person.bio: A few years ago, I decided to fully focus on cloud-native technologies, with one goal: to become a specialist in mission-critical platforms.  Today, I build, optimize and secure OpenShift and Kubernetes environments where reliability is essential. Along the way, I earned my Red Hat Certified Architect certification and completed the CNCF journey to become a Kubestronaut.  I don’t see certifications as the end goal. They are a confirmation of my expertise and a way to keep my knowledge up to date in a rapidly changing landscape.  I don’t know everything. Nobody does. But when I encounter something I don’t know, I dive in, figure it out and make sure I understand it. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and improve.  Automation first. Security first. Always learning. Always building. Always improving.

person.name: Karen Chu
person.bio: Karen Chu is an Apple OSPO PM focused on Swift language community initiatives. Having participated in the cloud native community since 2015, she is a CNCF Ambassador, Helm community manager/maintainer, emeritus Kubernetes Code of Conduct Committee member, meet-up organizer, and conference organizer. She has also worked on The Illustrated Children’s Guide to Kubernetes book series. When she’s not connecting dots in the community, you can find her pursuing photography, cooking, and sipping on chai lattes.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Having help build this community since 2015, I'm fascinated by how far the ecosystem has come and how much further it can go. I'm extremely excited to see CNCF participate in new spaces and so with my continued work as a CNCF Ambassador, I look forward to helping bridge the gap between the cloud native and Webassembly communities.

person.name: Adam Moszumanski
person.bio: Adam is an experienced IT professional with over two decades of experience across public and private sectors, including industrial, healthcare, and financial environments. He has held roles in software development, operations, and systems engineering, and currently leads a cloud platform team as both Engineering Lead and Product Owner. His expertise spans Kubernetes, AWS, cloud architecture, DevOps, cybersecurity, and large-scale cloud migrations.  Adam supports organisations in highly regulated environments by modernising legacy systems and implementing secure, automated, cloud-native platforms. He holds all five Kubernetes certifications, along with multiple credentials in AWS, cybersecurity and enterprise integration technologies. With a broad technical background, he brings a full-stack perspective to platform design, delivery, and transformation.

person.name: Junyong Kim
person.bio: I'm Junyong Kim, a cloud engineer working across various cloud platforms. I’m focused on infrastructure, automation, and security, and passionate about technical education.

person.name: Danilo De Oliveira Sousa
person.bio: DevOps Engineer based in Porto, Portugal, focused on cloud-native technologies and container orchestration. Skilled in managing Kubernetes clusters and building scalable, secure, and resilient infrastructure. Experienced with GitOps, observability tools, infrastructure automation, CI/CD pipelines, and other cloud-native and open-source technologies. Passionate about improving operational excellence and enhancing developer experience.

person.name: Daniel Krook
person.bio: Daniel Krook is focused on better serving the maintainers, contributors, and users in the community of 200+ open source projects hosted by the CNCF. He has been involved in the use, creation, and advocacy of open source technology for over 20 years. He partnered with the LF to establish the "Call for Code with The Linux Foundation" umbrella and has guided 16 projects through the process of joining the LF and using LFX tools.He has built software end-to-end for a wide array of industries and earned certifications in cloud architecture, application development, and system operations along the way. Most recently, he worked at IBM as a principal engineer developing first-of-a-kind solutions that integrate open source technologies with commercial products and services.Daniel has spoken at many open source conferences (including main-stage presentations at the first CloudNativeDay and the first KubeCon run by the CNCF), hosted dozens of meetups (primarily OpenWhisk, Cloud Foundry, OpenStack, and PHP), and has contributed to many technical blogs, videos, podcasts, articles, and books.

person.name: Ganesh Chavan
person.bio: I am cloud/SRE/DevOps engineer working with cloud‑native technologies on Azure, AWS, and Google Cloud. Hold a Microsoft Certified DevOps Engineer Expert certification and have strong experience with Kubernetes and modern DevOps practices. I enjoy learning new technologies and sharing knowledge to help others adopt cloud‑native and CNCF tools.

person.name: Lewis Bertin
person.bio: Based in France, I work extensively with Kubernetes and GitOps workflows, with a particular focus on Argo projects.  I'm passionate about cloud-native technologies and helping teams adopt modern deployment practices.  I enjoy sharing knowledge and exchanging best practices in a friendly way.

person.name: Marco Bulgarini
person.bio: As a Cloud Infrastructure Engineer, I take great pride in managing complex systems at scale, treating them with the precision and care they deserve.My passion for Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and GitOps drove me to Kubernetes and continues to push me on a path for more self-organizing, self-healing, self-scaling systems. Whether it's optimizing a Cloud Infrastructure with Terraform, fine-tuning a datastore or message broker, or instrumenting applications for maximum observability, I enjoy the challenge of making everything run smoothly.

person.name: Lukáš Šabľa
person.bio: Kubernetes wasn’t an instant love affair, but over time, it became a passion. I don’t believe everything is a nail—but Kubernetes is a really good hammer. I’ve been building and managing platforms since the days of PHP 4.3, mastering Linux, DevOps, and Kubernetes along the way. My journey took me through years at Slovakia’s largest hosting company, where I tackled real-world infrastructure challenges at scale. Now, with many years of experience, I design fault-tolerant infrastructures for high-traffic media, e-commerce, and on-demand video platforms, ensuring they stay reliable, scalable, and ready for whatever comes next.

person.name: Maximilian Matthies
person.bio: Computer Science Expert - Subject Area: System Integration (IHK), Trainer for Kubernetes@Cloudogu, System Engineer @ SVA GmbH

person.name: Yusuke Yokota
person.bio: I work as an IT architect specializing in big data technology. Using distributed frameworks such as Spark on Kubernetes and Kafka Streams, I am conducting PoC and building production systems for the process of accumulating large amounts of IoT data in a DWH in real time. In terms of cloud native technologies, my areas of interest are "Scheduling & Orchestration" and "Streaming & Messaging".

person.name: İbrahim Temür
person.bio: Senior Platform & DevOps Engineer with 15 years of experience in IT, passionate about Kubernetes, automation, and cloud-native technologies. Combining hands-on experience with a community-driven mindset to build reliable, scalable, and secure platforms. Proud to be a CNCF Kubestronaut, helping teams deliver faster and safer through automation and cloud-native practices.

person.name: Areg Hovakimyan
person.bio: Areg is a DevOps engineer specializing in Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. Working with multi-cloud solutions, including AWS and GCP, he use a wide range of CNCF tools daily, with favorites like Argo CD, Falco, OPA, and Crossplane. Focusing on building scalable systems that prioritize security while optimizing performance and resilience, he is passionate about automating processes to enhance infrastructure efficiency.

person.name: Deepesh Puthalath
person.bio: I'm Deepesh Puthalath, currently serving as a Technical Solutions Consultant at Hewlett Packard Enterprise. My professional focus lies in the exciting fields of AI and Machine Learning, where I work on supporting innovative solutions and driving technological advancements.

person.name: Mark Wilson
person.bio: Mark is a DevOps Engineer working in FinTech in Melbourne, where he plays a co-organiser role at K8SUG - Australia's largest Kubernetes user group. With a background in software development spanning over 6+ years, Mark combines professional expertise with a lifelong passion for IT.

person.name: Ore Akin-Odidi
person.bio: Technologist passionate about translating complex technical challenges into business driven outcomes, leveraging AI assisted development and DevOps tooling to deliver innovative, ethical solutions that drive revenue, cost savings, and long-term success.
